proposals.title: CSV output: always double-quote string/dictionary columns
proposals.detect: Validation shows column shift: value in col N appears in col N+1 in actual output. String column (e.g. tlabel) expected 'Equity, Including Portion...' but actual shows 'Equity' with remainder shifted into next column. Pattern: string_mismatch on multiple adjacent columns with no row-count error.
proposals.fix: Wrap ALL string and dictionary-decoded output columns in double-quotes: printf("\"%s\"", str.c_str()). Apply universally — never use bare %s for string columns in CSV output. Applies to any column whose dictionary or raw values may contain commas (label, description, name, tag, etc.).

proposals.title: Pre-built index header parse must be complete: cap + mask + ht pointer ALL declared before loop
proposals.detect: Compile error: 'is_mask was not declared in this scope' / 'is_cap was not declared in this scope' / 'is_ht was not declared in this scope'. File mmap succeeded but the three-line header parse block (cap, mask, ht) was omitted or placed in a different scope.
proposals.fix: After mmap of index file raw pointer, always emit all three declarations immediately: (1) uint32_t X_cap = *(const uint32_t*)X_raw; (2) uint32_t X_mask = X_cap - 1; (3) const XSlot* X_ht = (const XSlot*)(X_raw + 4); Place at function scope, never inside if-blocks or data_loading lambdas if the variables are used in main_scan.

proposals.title: TOP-N ordering: always include stable tiebreaker in sort comparator
proposals.detect: Validation: row count correct; first K rows match reference exactly; rows K+1...N contain the correct SET of entities but in wrong ORDER (permuted among themselves). Multiple output columns wrong simultaneously (name, tag, val). Breakpoint K corresponds to first rank position with tied aggregate values.
proposals.fix: Always append a unique stable secondary key to every std::sort / std::partial_sort comparator in TOP-N queries: if (a->primary_key != b->primary_key) return a->primary_key > b->primary_key; return a->entity_id < b->entity_id; — use cik, adsh, or dictionary code as tiebreaker. Also applies to ORDER BY in SQL logic translation.

proposals.title: C25 frequency increment
proposals.detect: Q4: 5/6 iterations returned rows_actual=0 with rows_expected=500. Filter elimination never recovered because no passing baseline existed.
proposals.fix: freq 2 → 3. Emphasize: without a passing baseline the optimizer has no safe revert anchor — first priority must be establishing a correct (even if slow) baseline.

proposals.title: C27 frequency increment
proposals.detect: Q6 iter_4: mmap of is_hash index performed correctly, but header parsing (is_cap, is_mask, is_ht) missing from scope where loop runs. Compile error 'is_mask not declared in this scope; did you mean fd_mask?'
proposals.fix: freq 1 → 2. The missing element in this instance was element (3) header parse — distinct from elements (1)-(2) and (4)-(5) which were present.

proposals.title: P17 frequency increment
proposals.detect: Q6: aggregation_merge 300-310ms across all 6 iterations. Thread-local merge never applied. Q24: aggregation_merge 60ms despite 6 optimization iterations.
proposals.fix: freq 4 → 5. aggregation_merge >100ms with no improvement across multiple iters = thread-local merge not applied at all.

proposals.title: P20 frequency increment
proposals.detect: Q6 aggregation_merge 300ms+ (shared map). Q24 final 60ms (partially improved but AGG_CAP oversized per P25).
